EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A potent storm system brought strong southerly winds ahead of it to White Pine county.

Other peak gusts included: 62 mph at the Alligator Ridge RAWS, 58 mph at the Ely airport, Murray Summit, and Pancake Summit.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large, cold, and slow moving system over the West brought gusty winds to western Nevada on the 15th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread wind gusts of 40 to 50 mph accompanied this system. Isolated high winds also occurred, with the wind prone Walker Lake NDOT sensor recording gusts between 60 and 75 mph with a gust to 80 mph at 0643PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A system off the northern California coast on the 25th moved into western Idaho by the 26th. This brought light precipitation to northwest Nevada with windy conditions further south.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread sustained winds of 30 to 45 mph occurred on the 25th. The highest wind gusts noted include 64 mph and 80 mph at the Schurz and Sweetwater NDOT sensors, respectively, and 71 mph in the Smith Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A system off the northern California coast on the 25th moved into western Idaho by the 26th. This brought light precipitation to northwest Nevada with windy conditions further south.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread sustained winds of 30 to 40 mph with gusts 45 to 55 mph occurred on the afternoon and early evening of the 25th. A few areas of high winds were also noted, including sustained winds of 40 to 50 mph with gusts as high as 66 mph for the Lovelock ASOS, gusts to 65 mph for the Dead Camel Mountain RAWS, and sustained winds of 40 to 52 mph with gusts to 64 mph for the Silver Springs AWOS.

CZ_NAME: LINCOLN COUNTY EXCEPT THE SHEEP RANGE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Yet another unseasonably strong Pacific storm brought high winds to south central Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds blew over an aluminum shed in Rachel, and blew away loose construction supplies, which took out a couple of communication antennas, at the detention center in Pioche.  The strongest measured gust during the event was 56 mph at Rachel.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Yet another unseasonably strong Pacific storm brought high winds to south central Nevada.

CZ_NAME: LAKE MEAD/LAKE MOHAVE NATIONAL RECREATION AREA
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of unseasonably strong Pacific storms produced spotty high winds in the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds on Lake Mead capsized and destroyed three boats during the Memorial Day weekend.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A small cluster of strong thunderstorms sparked a few brush fires in the southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning sparked three or four brush fires south of Highway 266.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A system off the northern California coast on the 25th moved into western Idaho by the 26th. This brought light precipitation to northwest Nevada with windy conditions further south.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread wind gusts of 45 to 55 mph occurred on the 25th. A few high winds also occurred, including 64 mph at the Washoe Valley NDOT site, 63 mph in northwest Reno, and 60 mph at the Carson Valley NDOT site and at the Western Nevada Community College on the west side of Carson City. Strong winds caused a tree to fall over onto a power line in Sparks. In Wadsworth, winds blew power lines together which caused two small fires with some building damage and an injury to a firefighter.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Slow moving low pressure off the northern California coast brought gusty winds to western Nevada on the 31st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread wind gusts of 40 to 50 mph occurred on the 31st. However, a wind prone area 5 miles WSW of Gardnerville reported gusts between 66 and 73 mph with minor roof damage (shingles torn off).

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds blew off the metal roof of a utility building next to the Elko Little League field. With the lack of nearby damage it was felt winds were sub-severe and the roof was probably old and flimsy.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm wind gust to 60 mph was recorded at the Bean Flat Monitor NDOT mesonet site.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lyon County Emergency Management reported a significant amount of water over East Walker and Pine Grove Roads with one truck in water up to the bottom of its cab. The deepest water was in the area of the Flying M Ranch.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very cold low dropped slowly south from off the Washington coast on the 18th before moving into northern California on the 21st. This brought gusty winds to western Nevada as well as heavy snow to the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread wind gusts of 45 to 55 mph occurred on the 18th. A few areas saw high wind gusts, including: 76 mph at the Galena RAWS, 65 mph in NW Reno, and 62 mph at the Reno-Tahoe airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very cold low dropped slowly south from off the Washington coast on the 18th before moving into northern California on the 21st. This brought gusty winds to western Nevada as well as heavy snow to the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread wind gusts of 40 to 50 mph occurred on the 18th. Brief high wind gusts also occurred in Silver Springs, with gusts over 60 mph between 1100PST and 1200PST with the highest being 67 mph at 1135PST.  A large tree was knocked down onto power lines in Fallon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Slow moving low pressure dropped down the Pacific Northwest coast and into California bringing heavy snow to the Sierra and portions of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall totals with this event include: 17 inches at Glenbrook (near Lake Tahoe) with 25 to 35 inches for Daggett Pass north to Incline Village and the Mt Rose ski area. In addition to snow, winds gusted over 40 mph for many areas from late morning through the afternoon on the 25th with gusts over 50 mph for a few locations including the Glenbrook Canyon NDOT station and the Little Valley RAWS. Over ridges, winds gusted over 80 mph with gusts as high as 110 mph for Slide Mountain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Slow moving low pressure dropped down the Pacific Northwest coast and into California bringing heavy snow to the Sierra and portions of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Five inches of snow was reported in Gerlach with this system.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Slow moving low pressure dropped down the Pacific Northwest coast and into California bringing heavy snow to the Sierra and portions of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread snowfall totals of 5 to 10 inches fell during this system, with 12 to 18 inches in areas west of Carson City and south and west of Reno (possibly enhanced by lake effect snow from Lake Tahoe). In addition to snow, winds gusted over 50 mph in the foothills and south of Reno from late morning into the afternoon on the 25th. Winds and snow in the Carson Valley caused 24 power poles to be broken or fall down onto both highway 395 and Muller Lane. In addition, several accidents caused at least two injuries in the Carson Valley. Finally, the snow was enough to cause Governor Sandoval to send home early non-essential state workers on the 25th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts ranged from 6 to 12 inches across the Ruby Mountains and East Humboldt Range.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Spotters and SNOTEL data reported 6 to 12 inches of snow fell.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several areas of cold low pressure moved from near the California coast through western Nevada the 23th through the 26th bringing periods of snow along with gusty winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two rounds of snow occurred with this storm, the first on the 23rd and the second from midday on the 24th through late evening on the 24th. The first round only dropped 4 to 8 inches of snow, however the second round dropped one to two feet. In addition to snowfall, gust winds of 25 to 35 mph accompanied the second round of snow on the 24th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast moving system moved from the eastern Pacific into Oregon on the 25th before moving into Idaho on the 26th. The system brought gusty winds to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread wind gusts of 40 to 50 mph occurred on the 25th. Isolated high winds also were noted in wind prone areas, including 60 mph at the Washoe Valley NDOT site, 59 mph in southwest Reno at the Wolf Run Golf Course, and 58 mph at the Galena RAWS. In addition to gusty winds, a report of minor wind damage to a roof in Carson City was reported.

Here are some specific totals: 7 to 8.5 inches in Silver Springs, 4 inches in Fernley, and 2 inches in Fallon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful storm system brought high winds to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The peak gust for this event occurred at McCarran Airport (KLAS) at 1236 PST.  Broadcast media, spotters, and the public reported damage in Las Vegas, North Las Vegas, and Henderson, including several trees down and at least two business signs damaged or destroyed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful storm system brought high winds to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two tents were blown over and damaged at the Clark County Fair and Rodeo in Logandale.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought strong winds to portions of eastern and central Nevada. Wind gusts up to 77 mph were reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Currant Creek NDOT Mesonet site measured a wind gust to 69 mph. The Currant Creek RAWS measured a wind gust to 59 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought strong winds to portions of eastern and central Nevada. Wind gusts up to 77 mph were reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust to 62 mph was measured at the Baker NDOT Mesonet site and a gust to 61 mph was measured at the Pancake Summit NDOT Mesonet site.

CZ_NAME: LINCOLN COUNTY EXCEPT THE SHEEP RANGE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful Pacific storm brought high wind to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin, along with high mountain snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 1 mile WNW of Rachel.

CZ_NAME: LINCOLN COUNTY EXCEPT THE SHEEP RANGE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful Pacific storm brought high wind to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin, along with high mountain snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 4 miles W of Pioche.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful Pacific storm brought high wind to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin, along with high mountain snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 1 mile ESE of Red Rock Canyon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful Pacific storm brought high wind to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin, along with high mountain snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred near the intersection of the 215 freeway and Cheyenne on the northwest side of Las Vegas. Several roof tiles were torn off, and one broke the sun roof of a vehicle.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A spring storm brought up to a foot of snow to portions of northern and central Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Eureka and Austin report 12 inches of new snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Winds gusting up to 70 mph occurred on the lee side of the Ruby Mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts to 65 mph were recorded by the Ruby Valley RAWS.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Winds gusting up to 70 mph occurred on the lee side of the Ruby Mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds gusting up to 70 mph were reported by the Ruby Lake RAWS.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought heavy snow to elevations above 6500 feet where up to 16 inches of snow was reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Many SNOTEL sites reported between 7 and 12 inches of snow. Wild Horse Reservoir reported 9 inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought heavy snow to elevations above 6500 feet where up to 16 inches of snow was reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts ranged from 9 to 16 inches at most SNOTEL sites.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought heavy snow to elevations above 6500 feet where up to 16 inches of snow was reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts above 6500 feet were estimated at 8 to 10 inches based on lower elevation precipitation reports from Coop Observers.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold system dropping south from the Gulf of Alaska brought a strong cold front through the region with heavy Sierra snow and strong winds for portions of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds gusted between 60 and 84 mph from the evening of the 15th through around 0100PST on the 16th as a cold front moved through the region. The high winds caused highway 95 to be closed between 1800PST on the 15th and 0600PST on the 16th. Specific wind gusts include: 73 mph at the Schurz NDOT station at 2342PST and 84 mph between 1720PST and 1823PST at the Walker Lake NDOT station.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought strong winds to portions of eastern and central Nevada. Wind gusts up to 77 mph were reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A gust to 59 mph was reported at the Wendover ASOS.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought strong winds to portions of eastern and central Nevada. Wind gusts up to 77 mph were reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Ruby Valley RAWS measured a wind gust to 77 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought strong winds to portions of eastern and central Nevada. Wind gusts up to 77 mph were reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts to 60 mph were estimated in Ruby and Clover Valleys based on the 77 mph gust measured at the Ruby Valley RAWS and a 56 mph gust at the Ruby Lake RAWS.

CZ_NAME: LINCOLN COUNTY EXCEPT THE SHEEP RANGE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold Pacific storm brought heavy snow to the higher elevations of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Locally heavy rain also occurred in the valleys.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter measured 9 inches of snow in Pioche (6000').

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold Pacific storm brought heavy snow to the higher elevations of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Locally heavy rain also occurred in the valleys.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter measured 11 inches of snow 5 miles SW of Spring Mountain Ranch.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought subfreezing temperatures to much of the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Subfreezing temperatures gripped the Las Vegas Valley for the better part of two days, with lows down to 16F in outlying parts of the city. The cold damaged components of several thousand residential pools, especially pressure vacuum breakers and anti-siphon valves.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front blew through the Mojave Desert, with strong west to southwest winds ahead of it and strong north winds behind it.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter measured a gust to 45 knots, which blew out a transformer, in the north part of the Las Vegas Valley at the onset of the winds.  The McCarran Airport ASOS (KLAS) measured a gust to 50 knots two hours later, after which the winds subsided.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful Pacific storm system produced high winds, including extreme downslope winds, and locally heavy high elevation snow over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six inches of snow fell in Goldfield.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific storm system and associated cold front brought strong west to southwest winds ahead of the front, and strong north winds behind the front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An off-duty NWS employee in North Las Vegas observed two 8 to 9 inch diameter mesquite trees which were split by the wind, and a stop sign which was bent over.  In Henderson, broadcast media reported a tree approximately 2 feet in diameter was blown over in a back yard.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold system dropping south from the Gulf of Alaska brought a strong cold front through the region with heavy Sierra snow and strong winds for portions of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a brief and isolated gust to 66 mph at the Reno Tahoe airport at 0800PST on the 14th but the most widespread high winds occurred on the 15th. Widespread wind gusts of 60 to 67 mph occurred in the foothills and into southern and western areas of Reno from late morning to early afternoon on the 15th. The highest wind gust occurred at the Galena RAWS with 85 mph at 0900PST.  Damage reports with the gusty winds include: an overturned semi truck near Verdi on the 14th, four to 6 inch tree limbs down near Gardnerville on the 15th, a power line down in southwest Reno which sparked a fire (no damage from fire).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The same cold trough that caused heavy snow less than 2 days before sent another wave of energy at the region for additional heavy snow. This system wrapped up a total of 4 to 6 feet of snow for the Carson Range from the 15th through the 19th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Daggett Pass CO-OP reported 19 inches with this round of heavy snow, with 30 to 40 inches at the Diamond Peak and Mt Rose ski areas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The same cold trough that caused heavy snow less than 2 days before sent another wave of energy at the region for additional heavy snow. This system wrapped up a total of 4 to 6 feet of snow for the Carson Range from the 15th through the 19th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A widespread 5 to 8 inches of snow fell for the Reno and Carson City areas, with 8 to 12 inches in the foothills west of highway 395. Lesser amounts of 2 to 4 inches fell east of Reno and Carson City, including Sparks and Spanish Springs.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The same cold trough that caused heavy snow less than 2 days before sent another wave of energy at the region for additional heavy snow. This system wrapped up a total of 4 to 6 feet of snow for the Carson Range from the 15th through the 19th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A widespread 4 to 6 inches of snow fell in about 12 hours. However, with temperatures hovering near freezing there was little impact through around 2000PST on the 18th. Chain and snow tire requirements did become widespread by the morning of the 19th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought winds gusts up to 74 mph across Elko county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust to 59 mph was measured at the Elko Airport, and gusts to 60 mph were measured by the Ruby Lake RAWS and on Pequop Summit by a NDOT remote sensor.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold upper low dropped south along the Pacific Northwest coast and into northern California from the 6th to the 7th, dragging a cold front with strong winds through western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds gusted over 58 mph the evening of the 6th into early morning on the 7th at the Walker Lake NDOT site. Wind gusts reached 70 to 97 mph between 2000PST and 0100PST, with the 97 mph gust at 2230PST. In addition to Walker Lake, the Sweetwater NDOT site gusted to 71 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold upper low dropped south along the Pacific Northwest coast and into northern California from the 6th to the 7th, dragging a cold front with strong winds through western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread wind gusts of 40 to 50 mph occurred on the 6th. Isolated high wind gusts also occurred in wind prone areas, including 59 mph at the Washoe Valley NDOT site and gusts 74 to 89 mph between 0100PST and 0300 PST at the Galena RAWS.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong push of monsoon moisture brought thunderstorms to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.  Several storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds separated three docks at the Callville Bay marina.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong push of monsoon moisture brought thunderstorms to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.  Several storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The same thunderstorm which caused widespread wind damage also caused serious flash flooding. One man who was rescued from a flooded wash suffered minor injuries. Several swift water rescues were needed near the intersection of Warm Springs and Pecos, and at least three feet of water covered the intersection of Warm Springs and Eastern, with several cars abandoned in the water. A spotter in Henderson measured 0.73 inch in one hour.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong push of monsoon moisture brought thunderstorms to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.  Several storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The peak gust of 64 mph occurred 9 miles NW of Mercury.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong push of monsoon moisture brought thunderstorms to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.  Several storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mud was reported over Highway 375 17 miles NW of Rachel.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong push of monsoon moisture brought thunderstorms to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.  Several storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Roads leading into Bonnie Springs and Spring Mountain Ranch State Park were washed out by flooding.  A co-op observer in the area reported 1.73 inches of rain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a strong thunderstorm caused one to two feet of debris to flow across State Highway 377 near Manhattan in northern Nye county. Water and debris were also reported in the town of Manhattan.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a strong thunderstorm caused one to two feet of debris to flow across State Highway 377 about 1/2 mile east of the intersection with State Highway 376. Water and debris also covered about 60 percent of the town of Manhattan.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold Pacific storm brought snow to the southern Great Basin and the higher elevations of the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred at Creech Air Force Base (KINS).

CZ_NAME: LINCOLN COUNTY EXCEPT THE SHEEP RANGE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold Pacific storm brought snow to the southern Great Basin and the higher elevations of the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Sheriff's deputies estimated a foot of snow in two different places on Highway 93 in the northern part of the county.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold Pacific storm brought snow to the southern Great Basin and the higher elevations of the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Las Vegas Ski and Snowboard Resort measured 15 inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An EF0 tornado touched down in a rural part of southern Humboldt county. No observable damage was found.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tornado was seen by several people and was photographed. It was visible for about 5 minutes and occurred in a mostly rural area. Eyewitnesses said the tornado skipped as it moved along. An NWS storm survey could find no visible damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong low pressure moved through the Pacific Northwest bringing high winds to portions of western NV on the 18th. One of the most expensive and damaging fires in Reno history to date developed in southwest Reno as power lines arced in high winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High winds started in the foothills late on the evening of the 17th, with high wind gusts becoming widespread over the rest of the area early on the morning of the 18th. Here are some of the highest gusts: 80 mph at the highways 50 and 395 junction in south Carson City, 79 mph at the National Weather Service, and 71 mph at the Wolf Run Golf course in southwest Reno. High winds caused the arcing of power lines which started the Caughlin Fire just after midnight on the 18th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong low pressure moved through the Pacific Northwest bringing high winds to portions of western NV on the 18th. One of the most expensive and damaging fires in Reno history to date developed in southwest Reno as power lines arced in high winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds gusted between 59 and 69 mph from Sweetwater Summit in far southern Lyon County north into the Mason and Smith Valleys (including Yerington). The strongest winds were along highway 95 near Walker Lake, where winds gusted between 80 and 94 mph from 1340PST and 1800PST. A number of trees were reported down in Lyon County, with a blown transformer with power outages in Wellington and Silver Springs.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong low pressure moved through the Pacific Northwest bringing high winds to portions of western NV on the 18th. One of the most expensive and damaging fires in Reno history to date developed in southwest Reno as power lines arced in high winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High winds caused a tree to fall on a power line sparking a rapidly spreading wildfire in southwest Reno around 0030PST. The 2,000 acre Caughlin Fire fire spread, sometimes up to 30 mph, and caused the destruction of around 30 homes during the early morning of the 18th. Over 9,000 people were evacuated from the fire area early Friday before being allowed to return Saturday. One man died while evacuating his home, a firefighter suffered second-degree burns, and 16 others were admitted to area hospitals for smoke inhalation.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moist low pressure off the California coast on the 5th moved inland on the 6th bringing widespread rainfall amounts of 1 to 1.5 inches to far western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The intersection of Whiskey Springs and Grass Valley Road had about a foot of water flowing over it. Standing water was also reported on other roads in the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A late season storm brought 6 to 9 inches of snow to the valleys and 1 to 2 feet to the mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The snow observer in Ely reported 9 inches of snow, and McGill report 6.5 inches. In the mountains 1 to 2 feet was report by|SNOTEL sites.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very cold low dropped slowly south from off the Washington coast on the 18th before moving into northern California on the 21st. This brought gusty winds to western Nevada as well as heavy snow to the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A band of snow stalled out between Fallon and Reno bringing several inches of wet snowfall to Lyon County. The Mason Valley news reported up to 5 inches of snow in the Mason Valley while a CoCORAHS report 3 miles SSW of Yerington reported 3.5 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong and wet flow from the Pacific Ocean brought heavy Sierra snow and gusty winds for western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread wind gusts of 40 to 50 mph occurred with this system. However, there were brief high winds in wind prone areas including the Walker Lake NDOT sensor with gusts of 66 to 77 mph and the Sweetwater NDOT sensor with gusts between 60 and 69 mph between 2000PST and 2200PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong and wet flow from the Pacific Ocean brought heavy Sierra snow and gusty winds for western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Fourteen to 21 inches of snow fell on the 15th and 16th north of highway 50 above 6500 feet with lesser amounts further south. Below 6500 feet, up to 5 inches of snow fell with much of the precipitation falling as rain. Here are some specific snowfall totals: Incline Village with 14 inches, the Mount Rose Ski area with 21 inches, Daggett Pass with 11 inches, and Glenbrook with 5 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several areas of cold low pressure moved from near the California coast through western Nevada the 23th through the 26th bringing periods of snow along with gusty winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread gusts of 40 to 50 mph occurred on the 26th. Isolated high winds were also reported, including 72 and 61 mph at the Walker Lake and Sweetwater NDOT sites, respectively. According to the Mineral County Independent News, the gusty winds near Walker Lake caused a semi truck to overturn and a house boat to be blown off its trailer and crushed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought strong winds to portions of eastern Nevada. Wind gusts up to 76 mph were recorded.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts up to 65 mph were reported at the Ruby Valley RAWS and up to 61 MPH at the Crane Springs RAWS. The strong winds|blew over a wood stove chimney and moved a shed on a cement slab about 3 inches at a residence near Ryndon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A system moving through Oregon and far northern Nevada brought gusty winds and light precipitation to the region on the 10th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread wind gusts of 45 to 55 mph were recorded on the 10th. A few high wind gusts occurred as well, including 61 mph at the Sweetwater NDOT site at 1900PST and 77 mph at the Walker Lake NDOT sensor at 1800PST. High winds at Walker Lake lasted no longer than an hour, but they caused the closure of highway 95 between Schurz and Luning (Mineral County).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A system moving through Oregon and far northern Nevada brought gusty winds and light precipitation to the region on the 10th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread wind gusts of 45 to 55 mph occurred on the 10th. A few high wind gusts occurred as well, including: the National Weather Service in Reno as well as the Galena RAWS with 70 mph at 1000PST, Desert Springs RAWS with 63 mph at 1400PST, 65 mph at 1050PST at the Wolf Run Golf Course, and 60 mph at the Reno-Tahoe airport at 1200PST.  Gusty winds caused the closure of highway 395 through the Washoe Valley in the evening. Meanwhile, wind gusts of 100 to 120 mph at Slide Mountain and Virginia Peak were recorded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of unseasonably strong Pacific storms produced spotty high winds in the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The co-op observer in the community of Goodsprings reported that the wind destroyed a carport and also picked up loose tin and plywood construction supplies, which caused minor damage to a couple of cars. A mesonet sensor 1 mile ESE of Red Rock Canyon recorded a gust of 64 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning from a thunderstorm over the McQueen High School soccer field struck between two sets of bleacher seats on the afternoon of the 24th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning from a thunderstorm over the McQueen High School soccer field struck between two sets of bleacher seats on the afternoon of the 24th, causing 4 injuries. Two of the injured went to the hospital and were in stable condition while two others refused treatment. One man had his hair singed and the left half of his shirt burned.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of thunderstorms formed south and east of Reno and moved north into the Reno-Sparks area early in the evening of the 30th. The line produced widespread wind gusts between 45 and 55 mph, heavy rain, and small hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain also accompanied the hail.

CZ_NAME: LINCOLN COUNTY EXCEPT THE SHEEP RANGE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought high northerly winds to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Damage occurred in many areas, and there was also isolated heavy snow in the high mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds damaged electrical transmission towers and blew down a fence in Lincoln County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Below normal temperatures for several days at the end of December and beginning of January led to the death of one person.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One person died of hypothermia in Las Vegas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought very cold temperatures to the Mojave Desert over New Year's weekend, then a trailing Pacific storm brought locally heavy low elevation snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Mount Charleston Fire Department measured 10 inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought very cold temperatures to the Mojave Desert over New Year's weekend, then a trailing Pacific storm brought locally heavy low elevation snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A locally heavy convective snow burst produced five to seven inches of snow in Pahrump (2600').

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought very cold temperatures to the Mojave Desert over New Year's weekend, then a trailing Pacific storm brought locally heavy low elevation snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Subfreezing temperatures gripped the Las Vegas Valley for the better part of two days, with lows down to 17F in outlying parts of the city. The cold damaged components of several thousand residential pools, especially pressure vacuum breakers and anti-siphon valves.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful Pacific storm brought high wind to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin, along with high mountain snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One spotter 3 miles S of Pahrump measured gusts up to 78 mph. Another spotter in Pahrump reported that the roof of her deck was blown off.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rains from thunderstorms caused flash flooding over portions of Antelope Valley. Portions of the Antelope Valley road was covered in water.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Spotter reported rivers running through their property. Later in the day they attempted to drive to Eureka but the Antelope Valley road was covered in water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds gusted to 64 mph at the Winnemucca airport.
Monsoon moisture lingered over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin, continuing to fuel thunderstorms.  Some storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds blew down four power poles in Logandale, knocking out power to about 500 homes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused debris and mud to flow over state route 377 between Manhattan and state route 376. Some washouts were also reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused debris and mud to flow over state route 377 between Manhattan and state route 376. Also some washouts on the road were reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low helped trigger scattered thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert.  One dissipating storm produced high winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three stations measured gusts of 58 to 61 mph from a dissipating thunderstorm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure moved southeast from the Gulf of Alaska on the 4th into the Great Basin on the 6th. This brought significant snowfall to the Sierra, mainly above 7000 to 7500 feet, with substantial rain in the lower elevations on the 5th and 6th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Mt Rose SNOTEL had an estimated 12 of snow from late on the 4th through mid morning on the 5th. Further south and east, amounts were considerably less with an estimated 3-6 for Daggett Pass (CO-OP) and Big Meadow (SNOTEL). The snow level for the bulk of the precipitation event remained between 7000 and 7500 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought high northerly winds to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Damage occurred in many areas, and there was also isolated heavy snow in the high mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High winds in Amargosa Valley blew down several trees and damaged some older roofs and metal buildings.

CZ_NAME: LINCOLN COUNTY EXCEPT THE SHEEP RANGE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought high northerly winds to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Damage occurred in many areas, and there was also isolated heavy snow in the high mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred in Rachel.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought high northerly winds to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Damage occurred in many areas, and there was also isolated heavy snow in the high mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds disrupted four electricity distribution lines in Las Vegas, knocking out power to about 5000 customers. Large trees were also knocked down near downtown Las Vegas and Summerlin. The Nellis AFB ASOS (KLSV) recorded a peak gust of 58 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought high northerly winds to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Damage occurred in many areas, and there was also isolated heavy snow in the high mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 6 miles west of Pahrump. High winds continued beyond midnight, causing damage in December.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought high northerly winds to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Damage occurred in many areas, and there was also isolated heavy snow in the high mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A total of 39 inches of snow fell at the Las Vegas Ski and Snowboard Resort. The snow start time was estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought high northerly winds to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Damage occurred in many areas, and there was also isolated heavy snow in the high mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High winds which began on the evening of November 30th continued into December 1st.  In the Pahrump area, a boat, RV, and greenhouse were damaged, numerous shingles were blown off roofs, power lines and a radio tower were blown down, and a carport torn down by the wind fell on a woman, injuring her.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought high northerly winds to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Damage occurred in many areas, and there was also isolated heavy snow in the high mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 44 miles ENE of Beatty.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Excessive Heat Warning criteria were reached in Las Vegas on four straight days as high pressure built in from the east. One man died due to complications from the heat.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High temperatures in Las Vegas reached Excessive Heat Warning criteria for four straight days. One man died due to complications from the heat.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Snowmelt from a much above normal snowpack combined with little reservoir storage (nearly full reservoirs) to cause minor to moderate flooding of lowlands in the Mason Valley from mid June through mid July.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snowmelt caused minor to moderate lowland flooding late in June and into early July in the Mason Valley south of Yerington with some backyards and crop fields flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of thunderstorms formed south and east of Reno and moved north into the Reno-Sparks area early in the evening of the 30th. The line produced widespread wind gusts between 45 and 55 mph, heavy rain, and small hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rainfall also accompanied the hail.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning caused wildfires burned around 205,000 acres and threatened the towns of Tuscarora and Midas. An unspecified number of cattle and horses were lost as well as a recreational vehicle.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning caused wildfires burned around 205,000 acres and threatened the towns of Tuscarora and Midas. An unspecified number of cattle and horses were killed according to newspaper accounts and a recreational vehicle was destroyed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning caused wildfires burned around 205,000 acres and threatened the towns of Tuscarora and Midas. An unspecified number of cattle and horses were lost as well as a recreational vehicle.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning caused wildfire burned around 205,000 acres and threatened the towns of Tuscarora and Midas. An unspecified number of cattle and horses were killed according to newspaper accounts and a recreational vehicle was destroyed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper low moved through northeast California and far northwest Nevada early morning on the 8th causing nocturnal thunderstorms to develop over the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter in Stagecoach measured a thunderstorm wind gust to 70 mph which caused a power outage and possibly the breaking of a utility pole.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold storm moved from the Gulf of Alaska through the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin, bringing thunderstorms and strong winds, with spotty damage reported. Heavy snow also likely fell on the unpopulated high mountain peaks.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Spotters reported flowing water on Stephanie Street between Russell and Sunset, as well as on Marks Street between Warm Springs and American Pacific. Broadcast media reported that the intersection of Tropicana and Jimmy Durante flooded so quickly that several drivers were stranded in their cars, and had to be rescued.  The heavy rain also damaged the soccer fields at Sam Boyd Stadium Park.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture lingered over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin, continuing to fuel thunderstorms.  Some storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm wind gust blew over a semi truck on Interstate 15 just south of Exit 112.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A non-severe thunderstorm caused some roof damage in Paradise Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds blew 2 small pieces of metal roofing off a home and an adjacent house trailer. One piece of roofing landed about 100 feet from the structures.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced wind gusts to 65 mph near the northern end of Ruby Valley.
A strong push of monsoon moisture brought thunderstorms to much of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.  Several storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds caused damage in a large area of the Las Vegas Valley.  The AWOS at Henderson Executive Airport measured a gust to 62 mph. The storm also blew over several mobile homes, blew down numerous trees (one of which damaged a car), blew down several power poles and lines, causing power outages for at least 10,000 people, and destroyed at least one fireworks stand.
